extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, whose antecedent is the conjunction of all 8 boolean inputs, including hyperkalemia. rec_drug_b is set true by r3, whose antecedent is just hyperkalemia. Whenever r2 fires, hyperkalemia is necessarily true, so r3 also fires. The two rules target different outputs (r2->rec_drug_a, r3->rec_drug_b), so priority/tie-breaking never suppresses one in favor of the other; both outputs are set independently. Setting all 8 of r2's conjunct inputs true (hyperkalemia, seizure_history, polypharmacy, pregnancy, prior_gi_bleed, peptic_ulcer, asthma, g6pd_deficiency) makes r2 fire (rec_drug_a=true) and r3 fire (rec_drug_b=true) simultaneously, violating the mutual-exclusion property that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. heart_failure is irrelevant (set false; it only affects rec_drug_c).
